get.description: Retrieve a top-level summary of session data by session ID.

schema.title: Response Get Session Summary Api V1 Session  Session Id  Get

head.description:
Check if a session exists by session ID.
Returns 204 No Content if exists, 404 Not Found if not.

post.summary: Create a new session with provided Session ID
post.description:
Create a new session using the provided session ID.
Returns 409 if the session already exists.

delete.description: Delete a session and all associated data.

schema.title: Response Delete Session Api V1 Session  Session Id  Delete

get.description:
List all jobs associated with this session.
Returns job IDs and their current status.

schema.title: Response List Session Jobs Api V1 Session  Session Id  Jobs Get

get.description:
Get the status of all documentation upload jobs for this session.
Returns information about queued, running, completed, and failed uploads.

schema.title: Response Get Documentation Upload Status Api V1 Session  Session Id  Documentation Status Get

get.description:
Retrieve all documentation items stored in the session grouped by document.
Returns list of document bundles with docId and all its chunks.

schema.title: Response Get Documentation Api V1 Session  Session Id  Documentation Get

post.description:
Upload, chunk, and process documentation in the session.
Creates a job and queues it for processing - returns immediately with job_id.
Each chunk becomes a separate DocumentationItem with source='upload'.
Application name and version are loaded from session's discoveryInput or scrapeInput.

schema.title: Response Upload Documentation Api V1 Session  Session Id  Documentation Post

put.description:
Replace all existing documentation in the session with a single uploaded file.
This clears all previously scraped and uploaded documentation.
Chunks and processes the documentation with LLM - returns immediately with job_id.

schema.title: Response Replace Documentation Api V1 Session  Session Id  Documentation Put

delete.description: Remove all documentation (both scraped and uploaded) from the session.

schema.title: Response Delete Documentation Api V1 Session  Session Id  Documentation Delete

get.description:
Retrieve all chunks for a single documentation document (doc_id).
Returns one document bundle in the same shape as export, scoped to one doc_id.

head.summary: Checks if documentation item exists by documentation_id
head.description:
Checks a single documentation item from the session by its UUID.
Returns 404 if the session or the documentation item is not found.

202.description: Documentation upload is still being processed

post.summary: Upload documentation to session by doc_id
post.description:
Process uploaded documentation file using LLM.
Creates a job and queues it for processing - returns immediately with job_id.
Each chunk becomes a separate DocumentationItem with source='upload' and the provided documentation_id as doc_id.
Application name and version are loaded from session's discoveryInput or scrapeInput.

schema.title: Response Upload Documentation By Id Api V1 Session  Session Id  Documentation  Documentation Id  Post

put.description:
Replace one documentation document (doc_id) with provided chunks.
Other documents in the session remain unchanged.

schema.title: Response Import Documentation By Id Api V1 Session  Session Id  Documentation  Documentation Id  Put

delete.summary: Delete all documentation chunks by UUID
delete.description:
Delete all documentation chunks with the specified doc_id from the session.
Since uploaded documentation is chunked, this removes all chunks belonging to the same document.
Returns 404 if the session or any documentation with that doc_id is not found.

parameters.name: documentation_id

schema.description: Documentation doc_id (deletes all chunks with this doc_id)
schema.title: Documentation Id

parameters.description: Documentation doc_id (deletes all chunks with this doc_id)

schema.title: Response Delete Documentation Item Api V1 Session  Session Id  Documentation  Documentation Id  Delete

post.description: Create a new session and return the session ID.

Enqueue a job to discover candidate documentation URLs for the given application.
The discovered URLs will be stored in the session.

get.description: Get the status of candidate links discovery job.

post.description:
Enqueue a job to scrape documentation from provided URLs.
The scraped documentation will be stored in the session.

get.description: Get the status of documentation scraping job.

post.description:
Extract object classes from documentation stored in or uploaded to the session.
Returns all extracted object classes enriched with confidence (high/medium/low)
ordered from highest to lowest confidence.
Returns jobId to poll for results.

get.description:
Get the status of object classes extraction job.
If jobId is not provided, retrieves the job from session.
Returns the current session data (which may include endpoints added after job completion).

parameters.name: jobId

schema.description: Job ID (optional, will use session's job if not provided)
schema.title: Jobid

parameters.description: Job ID (optional, will use session's job if not provided)

put.summary: Upload all object classes to session
put.description:
Upload all object classes to the session.
Expects a JSON body with objectClasses array.
Replaces existing object classes in the session.

get.description:
Get a specific object class by name from the session.
Returns the object class with all its data including endpoints and attributes.

schema.title: Response Get Specific Object Class Api V1 Digester  Session Id  Classes  Object Class  Get

put.summary: Upload one object class to session
put.description:
Upload or update a specific object class in the session.
If the object class already exists, it will be updated.
If it doesn't exist, it will be added to the objectClasses array.

post.description:
Extract attributes schema for a specific object class.
Only processes chunks that are relevant to the object class (from relevantDocumentations).
Updates both {object_class}AttributesOutput and the attributes field in the specific object class.

NOTE: We dont need to await documentation here, as it should have already been awaited during object class extraction.

get.description:
Get the status of attributes extraction job for the specified object class.
Returns the current session data (which may have been updated after job completion).

put.description: Manually override the attributes for an object class.

post.description:
Extract API endpoints for a specific object class.
Automatically loads base API URL from session metadata if available.
Updates both {object_class}EndpointsOutput and the endpoints field in the specific object class.
Only processes chunks that are relevant to the object class (from relevantDocumentations).

NOTE: We dont need to await documentation here, as it should have already been awaited during object class extraction.

get.description: Get the status of endpoints extraction job for the specified object class.

put.description: Manually override the endpoints for an object class.

post.description:
Extract relations between object classes from documentation.
Loads object classes from session.

NOTE: We dont need to await documentation here, as it should have already been awaited during object class extraction.

get.description: Get the status of relations extraction job.
Get the status of auth extraction job.
Get the status of metadata extraction job.

put.description: Restore metadataOutput in session from provided infoMetadata payload.

post.summary: Generate native schema for object class
post.description:
Generate native Groovy schema from attributes.
Loads attributes from session automatically.

parameters.name: skipCache

schema.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation
schema.title: Skipcache

parameters.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation

get.description: Get the status of native schema generation job.

put.description: Manually override the native schema for an object class.

post.description:
Generate ConnID Groovy code from attributes.
Loads attributes from session automatically.

parameters.name: skipCache

schema.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation
schema.title: Skipcache

parameters.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation

get.description: Get the status of ConnID generation job.

put.description: Manually override the ConnID for an object class.

post.summary: Generate search code for object class
post.description:
Generate Groovy search code for the given object class.
Loads attributes and endpoints from session automatically.

parameters.name: skipCache

schema.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation
schema.title: Skipcache

parameters.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation

get.description: Get the status of search code generation job.

put.description: Manually override the search code for an object class.

post.summary: Generate create code for object class
post.description:
Generate Groovy create code for the given object class.
Loads attributes and endpoints from session automatically.

parameters.name: skipCache

schema.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation
schema.title: Skipcache

parameters.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation

get.description: Get the status of create code generation job.

put.description: Manually override the create code for an object class.

post.summary: Generate update code for object class
post.description:
Generate Groovy update code for the given object class.
Loads attributes and endpoints from session automatically.

parameters.name: skipCache

schema.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation
schema.title: Skipcache

parameters.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation

get.description: Get the status of update code generation job.

put.description: Manually override the update code for an object class.

post.summary: Generate delete code for object class
post.description:
Generate Groovy delete code for the given object class.
Loads attributes and endpoints from session automatically.

parameters.name: skipCache

schema.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation
schema.title: Skipcache

parameters.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation

get.description: Get the status of delete code generation job.

put.description: Manually override the delete code for an object class.

post.description:
Generate Groovy relation code.
Loads relations from session automatically.

parameters.name: skipCache

schema.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation
schema.title: Skipcache

parameters.description: Whether to skip cached data for generation

get.description: Get the status of relation code generation job.
Health check endpoint to verify the service is running.

uri.title: Uri
uri.description: Base URL or URI template to call the API (e.g., https://host/api/v1).

type.description: 'constant' if same for all deployments; 'dynamic' if varies per tenant/installation; empty string when unknown.

BaseAPIEndpoint.title: BaseAPIEndpoint
BaseAPIEndpoint.description: Base API endpoint for the product. Distinguish between constant URLs and tenant-specific (dynamic) URLs.

integrationType.title: Integrationtype
integrationType.description: Discovery protocol priority: SCIM, REST, or DUMMY.

llmGeneratedSearchQuery.title: Llmgeneratedsearchquery
llmGeneratedSearchQuery.description: Use LLM to generate web search queries (default to use templates)

skipCache.title: Skipcache
skipCache.description: Whether to skip cached discovery output when input is the same.

enableLinkFiltering.title: Enablelinkfiltering
enableLinkFiltering.description: Enable LLM-based filtering of irrelevant links

enableLinkRanking.title: Enablelinkranking
enableLinkRanking.description: Enable LLM-based ranking of candidate links

numQueries.title: Numqueries
numQueries.description: How many distinct search queries to run during discovery (allowed range: 1-8).

maxResultsPerQuery.title: Maxresultsperquery
maxResultsPerQuery.description: How many results to fetch per query (per backend)

maxCandidateLinks.title: Maxcandidatelinks
maxCandidateLinks.description: Max number of candidate links to return after ranking/selection

maxFilterLlmCalls.title: Maxfilterllmcalls
maxFilterLlmCalls.description: Maximum number of LLM calls for filtering irrelevant links

currentScript.title: Currentscript
currentScript.description: Current user-edited Groovy script to repair.

midpointErrors.title: Midpointerrors
midpointErrors.description: midPoint runtime or validation errors returned for the current script.

preferredEndpoints.title: Preferredendpoints
preferredEndpoints.description: Optional user-provided preferred endpoints used to focus code generation.

Documentation.title: Documentation
Documentation.description: API model for one logical document containing all its chunks.

chunkId.title: Chunkid
chunkId.description: Unique identifier for the documentation chunk

url.title: Url
url.description: Scraped URL or upload file URI

DocumentationChunk.title: DocumentationChunk
DocumentationChunk.description: API model for one documentation chunk payload.

name.title: Name
name.description: Application/product name as stated in the docs.

apiVersion.title: Apiversion
apiVersion.description: API version string as documented (e.g., 'v1', '2024-05', semantic).

apiType.title: Apitype
apiType.description: API technology types. Allowed values: REST, SCIM. OpenAPI/Swagger should be normalized to REST.

baseApiEndpoint.title: Baseapiendpoint
baseApiEndpoint.description: One or more base endpoints/URI templates with their constant/dynamic classification.

InfoMetadata.title: InfoMetadata
InfoMetadata.description:
High-level product and API metadata extracted from documentations.
Focus on global application info, not per-endpoint details.

infoMetadata.description: High-level application and API metadata if discovered in the documentations. Null when unavailable.

InfoResponse.title: InfoResponse
InfoResponse.description:
Container for high-level API metadata. Return null/empty fields when unknown.
Use the alias 'InfoMetadata' for serialization if needed.

jobId.title: Jobid
jobId.description: Unique identifier of the created job.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Structured list of error lines/messages for pretty presentation. Each list item is a single error line. Backward-compatible with 'error' string.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Structured list of error lines/messages for pretty presentation. Each list item is a single error line. Backward-compatible with 'error' string.

progress.description: Multi-document progress (documents + current document chunk progress)

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Structured list of error lines/messages for pretty presentation. Each list item is a single error line. Backward-compatible with 'error' string.

totalDocuments.title: Totaldocuments
totalDocuments.description: Total number of documents to process

method.title: Method
method.description: HTTP method of the preferred endpoint.

name.title: Name
name.description: Stable machine-friendly relation identifier in lowercase snake_case (e.g., 'user_to_group', 'membership_to_project').

displayName.title: Displayname
displayName.description: Human-readable relation name shown to users based on documentation (e.g., 'User to Group', 'Membership to Project').

shortDescription.title: Shortdescription
shortDescription.description: One concise sentence describing the relation meaning, grounded in documentation evidence. Leave empty when no trustworthy short description can be derived.

subject.title: Subject
subject.description: Normalized subject object-class name (lowercase) selected from the relevant object classes list. The subject is the side that consumes/receives membership, entitlement, assignment, or access.

subjectAttribute.title: Subjectattribute
subjectAttribute.description: Attribute on the subject that points to object identifiers/references (e.g., groups, roles, projects). Can be a virtual attribute name when the relation is explicit only via inverse/query evidence.

object.title: Object
object.description: Normalized object object-class name (lowercase) selected from the relevant object classes list. The object is the target entity referenced/assigned/owned by the subject.

objectAttribute.title: Objectattribute
objectAttribute.description: Inverse attribute on the object that points back to subject identifiers/references (e.g., members, owners). Leave empty when not documented or not applicable.

RelationRecord.title: RelationRecord
RelationRecord.description: Relationship between two object classes discovered in the schema.

relations.title: Relations
relations.description: List of discovered relations with normalized class names and supporting fields.

RelationsResponse.title: RelationsResponse
RelationsResponse.description: Container for relation records extracted from a schema fragment. Return an empty list when none qualify.

applicationVersion.title: Applicationversion
applicationVersion.description: Application version. If omitted, discoveryInput.applicationVersion is used when available, otherwise 'current'.

skipCache.title: Skipcache
skipCache.description: Whether to skip already processed data from a different session when scraper input is the same.

ScrapeRequest.title: ScrapeRequest
ScrapeRequest.description: Input payload to start the scrape job.

sessionId.title: Sessionid
sessionId.description: The unique identifier for the created session

message.title: Message
message.description: Confirmation message about the session creation
